details.diagnosis: The task is to assess the likely AIE mechanism for a cationic, heavily substituted polyaryl chromophore. From structure alone, the current top1 is ICT and the top2 runner-up is TICT. A further credible portfolio member is neutral aromatic, while ESIPT is structurally disfavored and unknown is retained only as residual uncertainty. Top1 is only a working hypothesis because the molecule combines donor-acceptor substitution with many rotatable aryl groups, so both charge-transfer and twist-assisted alternatives are plausible before electronic-state evidence is collected. The visible uncertainty is mainly whether excitation is dominated by a more delocalized ICT pattern, a twisted charge-transfer tendency, or a more locally excited aromatic state. Shared prepared structure context is available and ready, including a selected conformer and prepared XYZ/SDF, so both specialized agents can work from the same baseline structure. Macro can provide deterministic registry-backed structural patterning and mechanism-screening context, but it cannot settle excited-state state ordering by itself. Microscopic can perform one low-cost baseline S0/S1 action on the shared structure and may reveal whether the lowest bright excitation appears CT-like or more locally excited, but it cannot in this round map conformer sensitivity, torsional pathways, or multi-step excited-state relaxation. The first round should therefore gather both macro and microscopic evidence because macro screens the mechanism portfolio from scaffold features while microscopic adds bounded electronic evidence on the same structure. The case remains in portfolio screening rather than pairwise contraction because at least one still-credible non-top2 alternative, neutral aromatic, has not yet been screened or ruled out.
details.task_instruction: Run one deterministic macro screen on the shared prepared structure and one bounded baseline microscopic S0/S1 action to reduce portfolio uncertainty among ICT, TICT, and neutral aromatic.

agent_task_instructions.macro: Current reasoning phase: portfolio_screening. Agent framing mode: portfolio_neutral. The current_hypothesis 'ICT' is only a provisional top1 for bookkeeping in this stage. Do not anchor this macro task to it as if the mechanism were settled. Screening focus hypotheses: TICT, neutral aromatic. Screening focus summary: The case remains in portfolio screening because ICT is the current top1, but TICT and neutral aromatic remain credible and neither has been directly screened. First-round macro and baseline microscopic work can only provide indirect evidence on geometry, brightness, and charge localization. Task: Assess macro-level structural evidence relevant to the current working hypothesis 'ICT'. Reuse the shared prepared structure context when available, summarize low-cost structural indicators only, and stay within current macro capability. Hypothesis uncertainty to keep in mind: ICT leads because the scaffold contains an obvious cationic acceptor and anisole-like donors, but the same scaffold is also highly rotor-rich and sterically crowded, so baseline evidence is needed to determine whether the low-lying excitation is primarily charge-transfer-like, twist-prone, or more locally excited.
agent_task_instructions.microscopic: Current reasoning phase: portfolio_screening. Agent framing mode: portfolio_neutral. The current_hypothesis 'ICT' is only a provisional top1 for bookkeeping in this stage. Do not anchor this microscopic task to it as if the mechanism were settled. Screening focus hypotheses: TICT, neutral aromatic. Screening focus summary: The case remains in portfolio screening because ICT is the current top1, but TICT and neutral aromatic remain credible and neither has been directly screened. First-round macro and baseline microscopic work can only provide indirect evidence on geometry, brightness, and charge localization. Task: Run the first-round low-cost S0/S1 microscopic baseline task for the current working hypothesis 'ICT'. Reuse the shared prepared structure context when available. Prioritize semi-empirical or otherwise low-cost bounded evidence collection, not heavy exhaustive geometry optimization. Report local microscopic results only, and do not attempt mechanism discrimination beyond current microscopic capability. Capability note: Macro can screen structural preconditions and mechanism plausibility using one deterministic registry-backed capability, while Microscopic in round 1 is limited to one low-cost baseline S0/S1 action on the shared structure. Neither agent can directly resolve conformer dependence, torsional relaxation pathways, aggregate packing effects, or full excited-state mechanism closure in this first round.

details.hypothesis_uncertainty_note: ICT leads because the scaffold contains an obvious cationic acceptor and anisole-like donors, but the same scaffold is also highly rotor-rich and sterically crowded, so baseline evidence is needed to determine whether the low-lying excitation is primarily charge-transfer-like, twist-prone, or more locally excited.
details.capability_assessment: Macro can screen structural preconditions and mechanism plausibility using one deterministic registry-backed capability, while Microscopic in round 1 is limited to one low-cost baseline S0/S1 action on the shared structure. Neither agent can directly resolve conformer dependence, torsional relaxation pathways, aggregate packing effects, or full excited-state mechanism closure in this first round.

hypothesis_pool.name: ICT
hypothesis_pool.rationale: Cationic fused aromatic acceptor with anisole-substituted aryl donors makes donor-to-acceptor charge transfer plausible, but the structure is also highly congested and rotor-rich so ICT is not uniquely established from topology alone.

hypothesis_pool.name: TICT
hypothesis_pool.rationale: Multiple aryl rotors around a strongly substituted exocyclic alkene can support twisted excited-state charge transfer and restriction upon aggregation, but the extensive conjugation and relatively high planarity proxy leave uncertainty about whether a distinctly twisted CT state is actually favored.

hypothesis_pool.name: neutral aromatic
hypothesis_pool.rationale: A large rigid polyaryl chromophore can behave like a mostly locally excited aromatic system with aggregation-enhanced restriction effects, but the built-in cationic acceptor and methoxy donors make a purely neutral-aromatic picture incomplete.

hypothesis_pool.name: unknown
hypothesis_pool.rationale: Only structural proxies are available at this stage, so mixed or ambiguous excited-state behavior remains possible.

hypothesis_pool.name: ESIPT
hypothesis_pool.rationale: No obvious intramolecular proton donor such as phenol or NH is present, so ESIPT is structurally disfavored.

hypothesis_screening_ledger.screening_note: Still credible because the molecule has many peripheral aryl rotors and a donor-acceptor motif, but no direct excited-state twisting evidence yet.
Still credible because the scaffold is large and aromatic, but no state-ordering or charge-localization evidence yet.
No clear internal proton donor-acceptor pair is visible from the structure, so the basic structural precondition for ESIPT is missing.
The scaffold gives enough directional clues to prefer named mechanisms over a fully agnostic label, but uncertainty remains nontrivial.

planner_raw_response.portfolio_screening_summary: The case remains in portfolio screening because ICT is the current top1, but TICT and neutral aromatic remain credible and neither has been directly screened. First-round macro and baseline microscopic work can only provide indirect evidence on geometry, brightness, and charge localization.
planner_raw_response.screening_focus_summary: The next bounded evidence should test whether the initial excited state looks charge-transfer-like versus more locally excited and whether the baseline geometry already suggests strong twisting propensity; this reduces coverage debt against TICT and neutral aromatic without prematurely collapsing to a single mechanism.

planner_raw_response.task_instruction: Run one deterministic macro screen on the shared prepared structure and one bounded baseline microscopic S0/S1 action to reduce portfolio uncertainty among ICT, TICT, and neutral aromatic.

agent_task_instructions.Macro: Using exactly one registry-backed macro capability on the shared prepared structure, produce a deterministic scaffold-level screening report focused on whether the molecule presents stronger preconditions for ICT, TICT, or neutral-aromatic behavior. Do not make a final mechanism decision. Explicitly comment on donor-acceptor partitioning, conjugation continuity, rotor density, steric crowding near the exocyclic alkene and aryl junctions, and whether ESIPT structural preconditions are absent. Frame the result as portfolio screening evidence and identify which still-credible alternatives remain open after this macro pass.
agent_task_instructions.Microscopic: Run exactly one baseline-only S0/S1 action on the shared prepared structure under the supported first-round policy: low-cost Amesp aTB S0 optimization plus a bounded S1 vertical excitation. Do not add conformer sensitivity, torsion scans, excited-state optimization, or sequential follow-up jobs. Report only local observables relevant to screening ICT vs TICT vs neutral aromatic, such as excitation energy, oscillator strength, and qualitative orbital/charge-redistribution character if available from the baseline output. Do not make a global mechanism judgment.

planner_raw_response.hypothesis_uncertainty_note: ICT leads because the scaffold contains an obvious cationic acceptor and anisole-like donors, but the same scaffold is also highly rotor-rich and sterically crowded, so baseline evidence is needed to determine whether the low-lying excitation is primarily charge-transfer-like, twist-prone, or more locally excited.
planner_raw_response.capability_assessment: Macro can screen structural preconditions and mechanism plausibility using one deterministic registry-backed capability, while Microscopic in round 1 is limited to one low-cost baseline S0/S1 action on the shared structure. Neither agent can directly resolve conformer dependence, torsional relaxation pathways, aggregate packing effects, or full excited-state mechanism closure in this first round.

hypothesis_reweight_explanation.ICT: Rises because the molecule has a strong cationic acceptor fused into an extended pi system and methoxy-bearing aryl donors that make charge transfer plausible.
hypothesis_reweight_explanation.TICT: Stays credible because several aryl rotors and steric congestion could favor twist-assisted charge transfer after excitation.
hypothesis_reweight_explanation.ESIPT: Falls sharply because there is no obvious intramolecular proton donor-acceptor motif needed for proton transfer.
hypothesis_reweight_explanation.neutral aromatic: Stays in the portfolio because the chromophore is large and aromatic enough that a mostly locally excited state remains plausible.
hypothesis_reweight_explanation.unknown: Remains limited because structural evidence points toward named mechanisms, but the current evidence is still too indirect for closure.
